intro_text: r_c wanders past an old badger sett overlooking a dried-up riverbed, only to freeze. There's new scent here, acrid and sour.
decline_text: Surely it must be nothing... Uneasily, r_c continues on.

success_outcomes.text: Feeling ever so alone on {PRONOUN/r_c/poss} solo patrol, r_c carefully sniffs around the mouth of the burrow, but the scent change is unmistakable — a new badger occupant has moved in. Creeping forward, belly flat in the dirt, they manage just enough of a glimpse to confirm the badger's presence before it moves and r_c shoots backwards, sprinting back to camp to warn everyone.

success_outcomes.text: Feeling ever so alone on {PRONOUN/r_c/poss} solo patrol, r_c carefully sniffs around the mouth of the burrow, but after a few moments, realizes that the scent is relatively weak. A badger was here, no doubt about it, but it doesn't seem to have taken up residence.  This will be important information to report back at camp.

success_outcomes.text: Carefully considering their options, r_c decides on stakeout, and watches from a shaded vantage point. When the badger pokes its head above ground at dusk, they're prepared, and dart off back to camp to warn everyone before the predator has blinked the sleep out of its eyes.

success_outcomes.text: This can't be left unknown. intro_text: app1 runs along in the heat of the desert day, cajoling app2, who's really not keen on this sudden adventure at an unreasonably hot hour.
decline_text: The apprentices are called back to camp to kitsit.

success_outcomes.text: Rounding a bend in the rocky path, app1 ducks under a thorn tree, and through a gap between two rock pillars, and app2 emerges into the alcove beyond with a gasp. A little hidden pool lies perfectly still and quiet in the shade, and both apprentices leap into the water with exclamations of relief.

intro_text: The patrol stretches, fluffing out their pelts against the coming night as the stars begin to wake in the sky. p_l hums thoughtfully. There's an old tale of hairless monsters that nevertheless stalk the freezing darkness, unnatural reptiles that lack the sun-worship that defines their kind.
decline_text: A cold wind whistles through the rocks, uncomfortable and cutting, and p_l shakes {PRONOUN/p_l/poss} head. This is no time for storytelling, {PRONOUN/p_l/subject}'ll wait until everyone is tucked into a snug den instead.

success_outcomes.text: Once, long ago, a desert Clan refused to patrol their border, fearing not their distant neighbours in a land too spacious for war. The chupacabra took advantage, sneaking through their lands undetected, sipping blood from cats at night. Even if c_n doesn't have enemy cats on their borders, that doesn't make the borderlands safe.

log.cats_from: cat_from enjoyed cat_to's story of the chupacabra on a patrol.

No, not a monster — mange, the hairloss that can leave an animal mindless with itch and reptilian in appearance. Worth thinking about.

log.cats_from: cat_from enjoyed cat_to's story of the chupacabra on a patrol.
cat_from was impressed by cat_to's insight about how the chupacabra myth may have originated.

fail_outcomes.text: p_l spins a chilling tale to the patrol, rumors of cats going missing in the frozen desert nights, or corpses found drained of blood, of a foul, reptilian horror... too horrific, in fact. No one needed quite that much gory detail, thanks p_l.

log.cats_from: cat_from was disturbed by cat_to's story of the chupacabra on a patrol.

intro_text: Today is a day for learning the best times to ambush lizards — early in the morning, while they're sitting on the basking rocks caught up in their daily ritual.
decline_text: app1 is also interested in this basking ritual, instead of in hunting lessons.

success_outcomes.text: As app1 stares at the lizards from afar, p_l tells the story of the dragons, who gave coveted fire and tried to eat the sun in their greed. The stars struck them down in their arrogance, stripping all their heat and flight, and now their long lost descendants come each morning to worship what they can never attain.

log.cats_from: cat_from enjoyed cat_to's story of dragons on a patrol.

success_outcomes.text: p_l makes sure app1 is focused. Their ancestors found that by setting up ambushes in the rocks before dawn, and then waiting, the prey would come to them. This technique is why it's so important to understand which rocks the lizards flock to.

log.cats_from: cat_from learned from cat_to about why c_n cats set up ambushes for lizards before dawn.

fail_outcomes.text: app1 is too impatient for this lesson, and runs off to try to hunt. The rest of the patrol wait, mildly irritated, until they hear a yelp. app1 has gotten stuck upside down in a crevice a lizard escaped into, and lifting {PRONOUN/app1/object} out takes the rest of the morning.

log.cats_from: cat_to's hasty actions wasted cat_from's morning and spoiled any chance of a useful lesson.

intro_text: As the patrol pads forward in the dusty landscape, they spot a ball wobbling along, pushed by a gleaming scarab. A little Khepri!
decline_text: Weird, but not dangerous. The patrol moves on.

success_outcomes.text: It reminds s_c of an old tale. Khepri the sun beetle moves the sun across the sky, hiding it away at night in his burrow and being reborn in the morning to push it along its tracks in the sky.

log.cats_from: cat_from enjoyed cat_to's story about Khepri the sun beetle.

success_outcomes.text: Scarabs bring luck, s_c says confidently. In times past, during period of great danger, desert cats would bring a scarab with them for luck, preferably a blue one.

log.cats_from: cat_from learned from cat_to about how scarabs are a sign of good luck.

fail_outcomes.text: app1 rolls {PRONOUN/app1/poss} eyes. p_l is talking about dung beetles. Khepri is a dung beetle. app1 doesn't want to hear about poop scarabs.

log.cats_from: cat_from was disgusted when cat_to tried to tell {PRONOUN/cat_from/object} about dung beetles.
cat_from was frustrated that cat_to dismissed scarabs as merely dung beetles.

intro_text: Come here a second, p_l knows a really cool feature tucked into this canyon. {PRONOUN/p_l/subject/CAP} lead the patrol down, stopping in front of the lower half of an eroding rock face. Look — bones.
decline_text: Weird, but not dangerous. The patrol moves on.

success_outcomes.text: They're not bone-bone, p_l explains, tracing a claw across the protruding skull. And they don't look like anything that currently walks the badlands. They're rock, but... bone-shaped? p_l thinks these were a monster, one cursed by StarClan to be entombed by the rock until they became it.

log.cats_from: cat_from was fascinated by the giant bone-shaped rocks that cat_to showed {PRONOUN/cat_from/object} on a patrol.

success_outcomes.text: Look at all the layers of rock above these, p_l whispers. How much time have these bones spent buried, only to be weathered out by water? What was this animal, nothing like a cat, what world did it live in, what wonders did it see? {PRONOUN/p_l/subject/CAP} {VERB/p_l/place/places} a paw on the skull's snout, staring into long dead eyes.

log.cats_from: cat_from was fascinated by the giant bone-shaped rocks that cat_to showed {PRONOUN/cat_from/object} on a patrol.

fail_outcomes.text: No one else is interested in the strange rock-bones, and crestfallen, p_l tucks {PRONOUN/p_l/poss} tail as the patrol trudges out of the canyon.

log.cats_from: cat_from was very disappointed when cat_to showed no interest in massive bone-shaped rocks on a patrol.

intro_text: app1 crouches low behind some grass, searching for the ideal prey to fall to {PRONOUN/app1/poss} deadly claws. In front of the mighty hunter, a brightly colored beetle alights on a rock.

success_outcomes.text: Ha! app1 pounces, the pathetic bug crunching underneath {PRONOUN/app1/poss} mighty blow, splattering and — ewwwww. Ew, ew ew ew, there's bug juice all over {PRONOUN/app1/poss} paws, between {PRONOUN/app1/poss} toes, EWWWWW!

Ha! app1 leaps, tail lashing furiously as the beetle buzzes under {PRONOUN/app1/poss} forepaws, claws sheathed. {PRONOUN/app1/subject/CAP} {VERB/app1/bat/bats} the bug off the rock and onto the ground, pouncing after it, jumping and twisting as {PRONOUN/app1/poss} colorful prey flits around {PRONOUN/app1/poss} head in confused, concussed beetle-behavior, unused to being an apprentice's new favorite toy.

s_c pounces, flipping the beetle with soft paws and flopping over onto {PRONOUN/app1/poss} back, watching the insect scurry from one paw to the other, so completely entranced by its color and tiny, perfect legs that {PRONOUN/app1/subject} {VERB/app1/don't/doesn't} notice {PRONOUN/app1/poss} own skill catching and keeping the bug.

fail_outcomes.text: While app1 is wiggling {PRONOUN/app1/poss} butt into the perfect pouncing position, the beetle flies off. Awwwww.

Slimy, yet satisfying! Curiosity completed, app1 wanders off — but has to report to the medicine cat den later, when {PRONOUN/app1/poss} stomach reminds {PRONOUN/app1/object} why cats don't eat random bugs.

log.cats_from: cat_from was amused to hear that cat_to hunted and ate a beetle, then got a stomachache from it.
